reviews.review_description:
Very nice graphics style, feels like I’m in another world! The sound is very atmospheric, and there is a lot of equipment to have fun with.

I also love that there are NPCs to meet in game. The level themselves are quite hard but it’s very fun finding the solutions to progress.

I hope the game will add more levels in the future!

In the description, it states you can play the game seated you can if you wish to play  as a midget. You will be unable to see what's on the tables, but you can see underneath.
Despite this, I entered the game, and the wall I spotted the word tutorial. Now, this may be a way to find the settings and an option for playing seated, but try as I may, I could not get into the tutorial. At that point, I gave up and played a different game

developer_response.body: sorry about that. we'll check that the tutorial works while being seated. we have played the game seated and it works fine.

reviews.review_description: Minimalistic graphics (think about Valheim) but first person view, and just the right amount of details. Great story telling, no distractive hud, just full immersion. There is a little steep learning curve in the beginning as the game doesn't gives you much hints nor a tutorial you just jump straight into the story & then action. Highly recommended for those who are enjoying this genre.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind review. There is a tutorial in the Inn but after your review we realized we made the sign for it too small so we increased the tutorial sign a lot. Hopefully you can see it now.

reviews.review_description:
Despite no longer being in early access it still feels like a very rough test, nowhere near ready to sell.

None of the interactions feel right. Everything from using the inventory to combat is very clunky. I didn’t make it to the end of the tutorial before deciding to refund. 

The next to the last straw was the crossbow. There’s no manual reloading, you just press a button. The arrows are barely visible and don’t go where you aim them. I tried to grip the front with my other hand. Two handed grip doesn’t work, it jumps the weapon to your other hand while opening the inventory.

The last straw was the combat tutorial. It’s difficult because the controls feel very awkward. Trying to grab a potion with the pop up menu during battle is an exercise in frustration.

I love the concept of this game, but in its current state it’s unplayable for me.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for your review! We’re sorry to hear that you didn’t enjoy the crossbow reloading mechanic. It was a deliberate design choice, but we may revisit it in the future. We aimed for a simple reloading system because the game becomes much more challenging beyond the tutorial, and you need to hold your shield in one hand while firing the crossbow with the other.

Regarding the two-handed grip, we didn’t include it because the shield plays a critical role in blocking attacks. The crossbow is accurate, but it doesn’t fire like a laser gun. Gravity affects the bolts, causing them to drop over distance, so you’ll need to compensate for that. Aiming takes practice, especially in VR, where steadiness is key. Personally, I find aiming with one eye helps. With a bit of practice, it becomes second nature, and mastering it feels very rewarding. We wanted to create a challenge that feels satisfying to overcome—admittedly, a bit old-school in approach.

As for the tutorial skeleton, we’re sorry it felt too difficult. It wasn’t intended to be overly challenging, but it sounds like your health got low, and you couldn’t reach your potion in time. Using your shield more often or dodging attacks could help. Grabbing a potion during battle is intentionally tricky; it’s part of our effort to add realism. In a real battle, it wouldn’t be easy to grab and drink a potion under pressure. This mechanic adds depth to the gameplay, encouraging players to strategize about when to recharge health—whether by potion or spell. Attempting it mid-battle, as you experienced, can be risky.

If potions were too easy to use during combat, it would lower the stakes. Players could simply stockpile potions and use them anytime their health dropped, diminishing the tension of the experience. Opening the inventory while avoiding attacks or during a brief pause makes it much easier to grab a potion.

We’re disappointed that you gave us a 1-star rating without progressing past the tutorial. It’s a bit like reading a restaurant menu and deciding the food is terrible without tasting it. Perhaps if you gave the tutorial another try, you might get more comfortable with the controls, and they could feel less clunky. That said, it’s entirely your choice.

Thank you again for giving our game a try!
- The Dungeon Adventures Team

reviews.review_title: A chill game to play by yourself.
reviews.review_description:
It has a retro feel and the trailer looked a little dark but I decided to try it anyway because I’m sick of multiplayer games that have no one on them. It was super easy to just jump in and start having fun slashing at monsters and shooting bolts from my crossbow. 
It had d&d style story about some guy you are trying to find and things lead to you eventually fighting a dragon. I died many times till I figured it out how to beat it. 
Bad points - menus are a little janky but functional.  The first 3 levels are pretty easy but then it starts to get hard. I’d love more spells to use and I’d like to find out what happened to the elf prince. After finishing all 13 levels I wanted more so I hope the developers come out with new quests to play.

reviews.review_title: Great game that takes you back
reviews.review_description: Such an underrated gem! Love the progression, different weapons and the vibe.

Honestly had a lot of fun with this game, easy enough to pick up and fun to play. Really great experience and one of the most underrated VR games I’ve come across. Highly recommend this game earned my 5 stars!

reviews.review_title: Needs to go back in the oven.
reviews.review_description:
When I discovered Dungeon Adventure on the store I was pretty excited. I'm a huge fan of fantasy, rpg's and the like. Another game of this genre is always going to be a good thing in my book. Sadly, this one just isn't ready yet. Sure, it's early access, but tje level of Polish is way under that in my opinion.

I love that you start in a tavern, and that the bar tender tells you the back story to your quest. No other similar games do this. That's original and very cool. However, once you get into the game it just doesn't impress, nor did I find it fun. 
The menu's are extremely cheap and feel like place holders. But coupled with the flat shaded polygon style of the game, could be considered retro? But then I would advise the developers to promote it as such: A classic styled PC  rpg bought into V.R. This would make much more sense and instantly pull the interests of a large group of people. Again, the trailer would need to relay this. 

Combat and navigation of weapons feels clunky and unintuitive. Weapons have no weight or feel. Very little sound design, the music in the cave sounds like 'Wish you a merry christmas'

Turning can be snap or smooth, which is great, but neither worked very well. The snap is too jarring and over sensitive, the smooth made me feel ill and I don't get motion sickness, having been playing V.R for many years. For now for me at least it will have to be a refund, but I'll definitely check back down the road and hope to find a much better experience.

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for the review. We improved the snap turn a lot so it should be much less jarring now. Thanks for bringing that to our attention. We should have more improvements when we do our full release next month. We appreciate the feedback and giving our game a shot. - Something Awesome Games

reviews.review_description: I had a blast playing this game! Exploring the maps was exciting, and using the crossbow to take down enemies was incredibly satisfying. I’d definitely recommend it!

Dungeon adventures is a fun and good looking dungeon crawler with RPG mechanics. It's a great game to play and a lot longer and much more content than I thought. I really enjoy it, and I hope they'll add more content in the future!

I am really enjoying this game. The combat mechanics are fantastic, I like to shoot the enemies with crossbow and then hide to avoid being shot. It's quite easy when you time it right. It's easy to see your health and mana bar just by looking at your wrists. The storytelling is great. The voice is very soothing.
